# Puca

> Puca is a live Dublin public transport map for buses, trains, and Luas, built for people waiting at a stop or platform who want fast arrival context.

## Primary Links

- [Live app](https://puca.dev/)
- [Markdown overview](https://puca.dev/about.md)
- [Full AI-readable overview](https://puca.dev/llms-full.txt)
- [Sitemap](https://puca.dev/sitemap.xml)

## What Puca Does

Puca shows live vehicle positions and stop context for Dublin Bus, Bus Eireann, Go-Ahead, and Irish Rail trains, plus Luas stop search and timetable-based arrivals. It is focused on answering a practical question: "I am here right now. Where is my bus, train, or tram?"

## Key Capabilities

- Live bus positions from NTA GTFS-Realtime vehicle feeds.
- Live train positions and station movement data from Irish Rail.
- Luas Green Line and Red Line stop search with timetable-based arrivals.
- Bus route filtering by operator, route, and direction.
- Bus stop search by stop number or name.
- Arrival boards that distinguish scheduled and running services where data allows.
- Focus mode for tracking a selected arrival on the map.
- Favorites stored locally on the user's device.
- Installable PWA shell with offline app assets.

## Product Boundaries

Puca is not a journey planner, ticketing product, fare app, account-based service, or generic timetable archive. It intentionally prioritizes live map context over multi-leg trip planning.

## Technical Context

Puca is built with Bun, Preact, TypeScript, Leaflet, NTA GTFS-Realtime data, Irish Rail APIs, generated GTFS JSON, generated Luas timetable data, and SQLite schedule artifacts. It is hosted on Fly.io.
